articles.title: Odisha Sizzles: Jharsuguda Tops at 43.3°C Amid Sweltering Heatwave
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds") Odisha
articles.full_text:
Odisha Sizzles: Jharsuguda Tops at 43.3°C Amid Sweltering Heatwave
Odisha experienced a severe heatwave, with Jharsuguda registering the highest temperature of 43.3°C. Other areas also saw soaring temperatures above 40°C, prompting school closures and morning classes in affected districts as authorities anticipate continued hot and humid conditions.
Heatwave conditions swept across Odisha, with Jharsuguda recording an alarming 43.3 degrees Celsius, the highest in the state, according to the India Meteorological Department (IMD).
The IMD reported that at least 14 areas saw temperatures exceeding 40 degrees Celsius, intensifying concerns among residents and authorities.
School closures and revised schedules were announced in several districts to cope with the scorching weather, while forecasts predict sustained hot and sultry conditions in the coming days.
(With inputs from agencies.)
- Districts

articles.full_text:
🔴Big Breaking ..ଜଳୁଛି ଓଡ଼ିଶା | Extreme Heat and Humidity Grips Odisha | Kanak News
Odisha is facing an intense and severe heatwave situation as temperatures rise sharply across both western and coastal regions.
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued yellow warnings for multiple districts including Sundargarh, Jharsuguda, Sambalpur, Bolangir, and Nuapada due to extreme heat conditions.
Coastal districts are also suffering from high humidity, making conditions even more unbearable for residents.
According to forecasts, this scorching weather will continue till the 22nd, with no immediate relief expected. However, temperatures may slightly drop by 2–3°C after the 24th.
In response, the state government has announced strict heatwave guidelines, including restrictions on working hours, transportation rules, and construction activities. School holidays have also been declared in several districts.
Authorities are planning to set up heatwave relief shelters similar to cyclone shelters to protect people from extreme conditions.

articles.title: Heatwave Persists in Odisha, Jharsuguda Records State's Highest Temperature at 43.3°C - prameyanews.com
articles.search_term: heatwave (Angul OR Balangir OR Balasore OR Bargarh OR Bhadrak OR Boudh OR Cuttack OR Deogarh OR Dhenkanal OR Gajapati OR Ganjam OR Jagatsinghapur OR Jajpur OR Jharsuguda OR Kalahandi OR Kandhamal OR Kendrapara OR "Kendujhar (Keonjhar)" OR Khordha OR Koraput OR Malkangiri OR Mayurbhanj OR Nabarangpur OR Nayagarh OR Nuapada OR Puri OR Rayagada OR Sambalpur OR Sonepur OR Sundargarh) Odisha
articles.full_text:
Bhubaneswar, April 19: A relentless heatwave continues to grip Odisha, with Jharsuguda reporting a sweltering 43.3 degrees Celsius, the highest temperature recorded in the state, as per the India Meteorological Department (IMD).
The IMD noted that more than 14 locations across the state surpassed the 40°C mark, sparking heightened concerns among residents and local authorities alike.
In response to the severe weather, several districts have declared school closures and adjusted working hours to mitigate the effects of the intense heat. Meteorologists warn that hot and humid conditions are likely to persist in the region in the coming days.
